items.title: Gala gain upper hand in 1st leg win over Liverpool
items.summary: Arne Slot's 100th match as Liverpool boss ended in defeat as Galatasaray capitalised on a sloppy display to win the first leg of this Champions League last-16 tie 1-0 on a deafening evening in Istanbul.

items.title: Arteta: Arsenal in Europe's elite, must prove it
items.summary: Mikel Arteta said he believes Arsenal belong at Europe's top table, but urged his team to prove their Champions League credentials against Bayer Leverkusen.

items.title: U.S.'s Poch won't rule out Madrid: 'In due time'
items.summary: Mauricio Pochettino refused to rule out a move to Real Madrid on Tuesday, after ESPN reported that the United States men's national team coach was on the club's shortlist to take charge this summer.

items.title: Mexico grants visas to some Iraq players for WCQ
items.summary: Mexico has begun granting visas to players from Iraq's national team ahead of their World Cup playoff match scheduled March 31 in Monterrey.

items.title: Tudor zet fout recht na horrorstart Spurs: blunderkeeper vroeg gewisseld
items.summary: Tottenham Hotspur beleefde dinsdagavond een rampzalige start aan het Champions League-duel met Atlético Madrid. In de Spaanse hoofdstad stonden de Spurs binnen een kwartier met 3-0 achter, waarna manager Igor Tudor besloot om doelman Guglielmo Vicario erin te brengen.

items.title: Atlético v Tottenham, Newcastle v Barcelona: Champions League last-16 first legs – live
items.summary: ⚽ Champions League news from the 8pm GMT kick-offs ⚽ Galatasaray v Liverpool – live | Today’s Football Daily An early goal from Mario Lemina gave Galatasaray a first-leg victory in Turkey. Scott Murray was watching. Galatasaray still lead Liverpool 1-0 in Turkey. You can follow the last five minutes (plus stoppage time) with Scott Murray. Continue reading...

items.title: Two more Iranians seek asylum in Australia after football team flies out – reports
items.summary: At least one player reportedly refused to board plane home Team’s departure for Malaysia ends Women’s Asian Cup saga Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcast Two more members of the Iranian women’s football team have reportedly sought asylum in Australia after at least one refused to board a flight back to their home country after competing in the Women’s Asian Cup. A plane left Sydney airport on Tuesday night local time for Malaysia, reports said, with players and staff, ending a dramatic two days when five players were granted asylum after refusing to return home. Continue reading...

items.title: Champions League live updates: Newcastle host Barça, Atleti routing Spurs
items.summary: Enjoy all the updates from Tuesday's UEFA Champions League round of 16 matches, including Newcastle vs. Barcelona and Atlético Madrid vs. Tottenham.

items.title: VI Live: XXL-horroravond voor Tottenham Hotspur
items.summary: Dinsdagavond gaat de achtste finale van de Champions League van start. Onder meer het Liverpool van Arne Slot, Bayern München en Barcelona komen in actie. Mis niets met deze VI Live.

items.title: Nottingham doet Europa League-tickets in de uitverkoop om stadion vol te krijgen
items.summary: Nottingham Forest geeft korting op kaartje voor het Europa League-treffen met FC Midtjylland. De wedstrijd op The City Ground is nog niet uitverkocht en dus zijn alle toegangsbewijzen nu voor tien pond minder te verkrijgen.

items.title: Familiar tale for Slot after Lemina gives Galatasaray edge over Liverpool
items.summary: The good news for Liverpool is that the situation is salvageable, when it really might not have been. The bad news is that they were distinctly second best for the first three quarters of the first leg of their Champions League last-16 tie. Nobody who saw their second half collapse away to Juventus in the playoff round could be confident Galatasaray are a team capable of squeezing the life out of the second leg. There is a nervousness about them at the back, a persistent sense of misfortune about to strike, but going forward they are breezy, quick and fun. Their only regret will be that, having taken an early lead through the former Wolves midfielder Mario Lemina, they did not add a second goal to give them more to defend at Anfield. Continue reading...

items.title: Laboured Liverpool defeated by Galatasaray in Istanbul again
items.summary: Arne Slot's 100th game as Liverpool boss ended miserably as his side lost at Galatasaray for the second time in this season's Champions League campaign.

items.title: Galatasaray verslaat Liverpool en gaat met voorsprong return op Anfield in
items.summary: Galatasaray heeft dinsdagavond afgerekend met Liverpool in de heenwedstrijd van de achtste finales van de Champions League. De Turkse topclub versloeg de Engelsen na een intens voetbalgevecht. Een vroege treffer was genoeg om volgende week met een voorsprong af te reizen naar Anfield voor de return.

items.title: Waar de oplossing voor Liverpool lag tegen Galatasaray
items.summary: Galatasaray heeft thuis opnieuw gewonnen van Liverpool: 1-0. Tactische analyse van een duel waarin het elftal van Arne Slot voor de verandering wordt afgetroefd door een tegenstander met lef.

items.title: McIlroy set for late arrival at The Players after 'stubborn' back injury
items.summary: Rory McIlroy is expected to be fit to feature in his title defence at The Players but looks set to head into the tournament without a practice round at TPC Sawgrass.

items.title: Cheltenham Festival day two tips: 66/1 pick in the Turners!
items.summary: The Cheltenham Festival is always a pleasure no matter what, but isn’t it great to see such big fields and competitive races at the meeting this year?

items.title: Joey Barton refused bail after 'golf club attack'
items.summary: The former footballer has been remanded in custody and will next appear in court on 7 April.

items.title: Lossiemouth crowned new Queen of Cheltenham with Champion Hurdle triumph
items.summary: Lossiemouth swept aside all rivals to claim a brilliant victory in the Unibet Champion Hurdle at the Cheltenham Festival.

items.title: João Pedro shining brightest as Chelsea brace for PSG reunion
items.summary: Liam Rosenior has unlocked the forward and must do the same with Cole Palmer for the team to reach their potential João Pedro had been a Chelsea player for less than two weeks when he faced Paris Saint-Germain in the final of the Club World Cup last summer. The settling-in period was intense. The forward had an impactful substitute appearance when he made his debut in the quarter-final win over Palmeiras, struck a clinical double when Fluminense were downed in the semi-finals and then, on a thrilling, sweaty afternoon in New Jersey, delivered the coup de grace when Chelsea became world champions thanks to a stunning demolition of PSG. It was 3-0 when João Pedro lifted a clever finish over Gianluigi Donnarumma in the 43rd minute, and the manner of the humiliation was hard for PSG to accept. Heads were scrambled as the newly crowned European champions felt their aura of invincibility ebb away at the end of an epic season. João Neves was shown a red card for a tangle with Marc Cucurella – who else? – and the loss of discipline even involved Luis Enrique, the PSG manager, appearing to slap João Pedro in the face when a mass brawl broke out at full-time. Continue reading...

items.title: England's Walker retires from international football
items.summary: Experienced England defender Kyle Walker retires from international football with immediate effect.

items.title: 'Saddened' O'Neill fears impact on derby away fans
items.summary: "Saddened" Martin O'Neill fears larger allocation of away fans at meetings of Celtic and Rangers "may now not happen anymore".

items.title: England make one change for final Six Nations match against France
items.summary: England have made just one change to their starting line-up to face France in their final Six Nations match on Saturday as Ollie Chessum comes in for Sam Underhill.

items.title: ‘So much disrespect’: outrage grows over postponement of Women’s Africa Cup of Nations
items.summary: Players and coaches demand more accountability from Caf after latest decision further disrupts preparation schedule On 13 February, Patrice Motsepe, the president of the Confederation of African Football (Caf), promised that this year’s Women’s Africa Cup of Nations (Wafcon), scheduled to be played in Morocco between 17 March and 4 April, would go ahead as planned. One of the reasons he had to make that statement was the 2024 tournament had been postponed for a remarkable 19 months, until July 2025. That supposedly solemn presidential promise was broken on 5 March, 12 days before the start of the tournament, with many of the teams – including Nigeria, the defending champions, Cameroon and Ghana – playing friendlies across Africa and Asia to prepare for the showpiece, which also determines which teams get to represent the continent at next year’s World Cup. This is an extract from our free email about women’s football, Moving the Goalposts. To get the full edition, visit this page and follow the instructions. Moving the Goalposts is delivered to your inboxes every Tuesday and Thursday. Continue reading...

items.title: Wouter Vrancken legt uit waarom deze speler nooit start bij STVV: "Dan kan hij pas brokken maken"
items.summary: Kaito Matsuzawa kroonde zich afgelopen weekend tot matchwinnaar bij STVV tegen Cercle Brugge. De Japanner scoorde de beslissende 2-1 en bevestigde zo zijn sterke invalbeurt van een week eerder tegen Antwerp.

items.title: Verrassing! Opvolger van KV Oostende stuurt trainer door, bekende kop neemt mee over
items.summary: Dieter Vandendriessche is niet langer trainer van KV Diksmuide Oostende. De club, opvolger van KV Oostende, bedankte hem vandaag voor bewezen diensten.

items.title: Liverpool chute à Istanbul : Galatasaray prend une option pour les quarts de finale de Ligue des champions
items.summary: Galatasaray a pris l'avantage face à Liverpool 1-0 lors du huitième de finale aller de Ligue des champions.

items.title: L'Iran menace de boycotter le Mondial : les Diables Rouges pourraient en subir les conséquences
items.summary: Le Mondial 2026 pourrait être perturbé avant le coup d'envoi. L'Iran n'exclut pas un boycott, une décision qui pourrait rebattre les cartes pour la Belgique.

items.title: Chatelle ziet niet STVV, maar andere club verrassen in titelstrijd: "Niks te verliezen"
items.summary: Nog twee speeldagen staan er op de agenda in de reguliere competitie. Daarna spelen zes ploegen voor de landstitel in de Jupiler Pro League.

items.title: Verhaal RWDM Brussels lijkt weeral af te lopen: "Ik hoor dat ze het stamnummer van Doornik willen kopen"
items.summary: De toekomst van RWDM Brussels hangt opnieuw aan een zijden draadje. De vrees groeit dat de club haar financiële verplichtingen niet kan nakomen, met een faillissement tot gevolg. Voor DAZN-commentator James Odvart, die de reeks op de voet volgt, ziet het er allesbehalve rooskleurig uit.

items.title: Ajax zit met een dik probleem door... Harry Styles
items.summary: Ajax staat na de voorbije speeldag vijfde in de Eredivisie en riskeert daardoor de play-offs om Europees voetbal te moeten spelen. Precies in die periode is de Johan Cruijff ArenA echter volledig gereserveerd voor een reeks concerten van Harry Styles.
